records.ety: [L. aequatus, p. p. of aequare to make level or equal, fr. aequus level, equal. See Equal.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Equated; p. pr. & vb. n. Equating.]

defs: To make equal; to reduce to an average; to make such an allowance or correction in as will reduce to a common standard of comparison; to reduce to mean time or motion; as, to equate payments; to equate lines of railroad for grades or curves; equated distances.
quotes: Palgrave gives both scrolle and scrowe and equates both to F[rench] rolle.
